steps.title: Find the growth multiplier for 18 months
steps.content:
Convert the time period: 18 months = 1.5 years.

The exponent in $P(t) = 290(1.04)^{(4/6)t}$ has coefficient $(4/6) = (2/3)$, meaning for every year of time, the exponent of 1.04 increases by $2/3$.

Over 1.5 years, the exponent increases by: $$(2/3) \times 1.5 = \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{3}{2} = 1$$
Therefore, the population is multiplied by $(1.04)^1 = 1.04$ every 18 months.

steps.title: Convert multiplier to percent increase
steps.content: A growth multiplier of 1.04 represents a percent increase of: $$(1.04 - 1) \times 100\% = 0.04 \times 100\% = 4\%$$ Thus $n = 4$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
